cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the author's view on high school teachers' salaries. I need to look at the part where the author talks about teachers. The author says: "The high school students of today are America's future. ... America's future depends greatly on the high school teachers of America. ... it is sad that teacher's salaries are where they are. ... I do strongly believe that action should be taken to increase the salaries of teachers because they have one of the most important jobs in America." So the main point here is that the author thinks teachers' salaries are too low and should be increased. The author also mentions that CEOs are getting bonuses while teachers aren't, but it's not saying teachers should be paid the same as CEOs. The options are A, B, C, D. Looking at the choices: A) Teachers' salaries are not high enough. That matches with the author's statement about action needing to be taken to increase them. B) Salaries should be as high as CEOs. The author says they shouldn't be the same, so B is wrong. C) Teachers work harder than CEOs. The author says teachers work harder every day, but the point is about the disparity in pay, not that they work harder. But the option says "teachers work harder than CEOs", which is mentioned, but the main idea is about salary. The question is about what the author thinks when talking about teachers' salaries. The author's main point is about increasing salaries, not comparing work effort. D) Teaching is more important than any other job. The author says it's one of the most important jobs, not more important than all
